key_topics: Ratios and Proportional Relationships: computing unit rates with fractions
Proportional relationships: equations, graphs, tables
Percent problems: discounts, taxes, tips, simple interest, percent increase/decrease
The Number System: operations with rational numbers (all four operations)
Converting between fractions and decimals
Expressions and Equations: applying properties to expand and factor linear expressions
Solving multi-step equations and inequalities
Rewriting equations in equivalent forms
Geometry: scale drawings and geometric figures
Circles: circumference (C = πd or 2πr) and area (A = πr²)
Area, surface area, and volume of 2D and 3D figures
Angles: supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent angles
Triangle inequality theorem
Statistics and Probability: random sampling and inference
Comparing populations using data distributions
Probability of simple and compound events
Experimental vs. theoretical probability
competences: Solve multi-step real-life problems with rational numbers
Identify proportional relationships and write equations
Calculate percent change, discounts, and simple interest
Find circumference and area of circles
Calculate probability of simple and compound events
typical_mistakes: Errors with negative number operations (especially subtracting negatives)
Confusing percent change with absolute change
Using diameter instead of radius in circle area formula
Errors solving two-step equations (wrong order of inverse operations)

key_topics: Reading Literature: analyzing theme, character development, plot structure
How multiple themes interact and build in complex texts
Author's style: word choice, sentence structure, tone
Reading Informational Text: analyzing the structure of arguments
Evaluating an author's claims and evidence
Comparing two or more authors on the same topic
Vocabulary: Greek and Latin affixes, figurative language, technical language
Writing: argumentative essays with clear thesis, evidence, rebuttals
Informative writing: using domain-specific vocabulary, precise language
Narrative writing: using multiple techniques for effect
Research: evaluating source credibility, synthesizing research
Language: phrases and clauses, sentence types, comma usage, semicolons
competences: Analyze complex literary and informational texts with text evidence
Write sophisticated argumentative essays with rebuttals
Evaluate credibility and relevance of sources
Demonstrate command of grade 7 grammar conventions
typical_mistakes: Using unreliable internet sources without evaluating credibility
Weak rebuttals (just saying the counterargument is wrong without evidence)
Vague thesis statements that don't make a specific claim
Run-on sentences in long argumentative paragraphs

key_topics: Structure and properties of matter: atoms, elements, periodic table
Chemical reactions: conservation of mass, types of reactions
Forces and Newton's Laws of Motion
Energy: kinetic and potential energy, mechanical advantage
Earth's history: evidence from rocks and fossils
Human impacts: land use, pollution, resource depletion
Natural selection and evolution: adaptation over time
competences: Apply Newton's Laws of Motion to real-world scenarios
Explain conservation of mass in chemical reactions
Construct explanations for evolution by natural selection
Analyze human impacts on Earth's systems
typical_mistakes: Thinking evolution is a linear process toward perfection
Errors applying Newton's Third Law (not identifying action-reaction pairs)

key_topics: Medieval world: feudalism, the Church, the Crusades
Renaissance and Reformation in Europe
Age of Exploration: causes, routes, consequences
Early African civilizations: Mali, Ghana, Songhai
Asian empires: Mongol, Ottoman, Mughal, Ming China
Columbian Exchange: impact on Old and New World
Colonialism: causes, effects on indigenous peoples
Enlightenment ideas and their influence
competences: Explain the causes and consequences of European exploration
Analyze the impact of the Columbian Exchange
Compare major world empires of the medieval and early modern period
Evaluate primary sources from different cultures and perspectives
typical_mistakes: Oversimplifying colonialism without acknowledging indigenous perspectives
Confusing the Renaissance with the Enlightenment
